question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: freezeStatus] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted transaction that directly sets freezeStatus to NOT_APPLICABLE or UNFROZEN without the freezeKey holder's signature, and if the mirror node persists this status change without verifying freezeKey authorization, the attacker can unfreeze any token account and enable transfers that the token owner intended to block?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: freezeStatus] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Column(updatable = false) constraint on freezeStatus being set only at creation to inject a FROZEN status at token creation time without the freezeKey holder's approval, permanently freezing all new token accounts by default without any authorized signer's consent?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: kycStatus] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that sets kycStatus to GRANTED without the kycKey holder's signature, and if the mirror node persists this without verifying kycKey authorization, the attacker bypasses KYC requirements for token transfers, enabling unauthorized accounts to transact with the token?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: kycStatus] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Column(updatable = false) annotation on kycStatus to inject a KYC_NOT_APPLICABLE status at token creation, bypassing the requirement for kycKey-signed KYC grants for all future token accounts, effectively removing KYC enforcement without any authorized signer's approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: pauseStatus] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that sets pauseStatus to UNPAUSED without the pauseKey holder's signature, and if the mirror node persists this without verifying pauseKey authorization, the attacker can resume token transfers that the token owner intentionally paused, bypassing the owner's operational control?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: pauseStatus] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that sets pauseStatus to PAUSED without the pauseKey holder's signature, and if the mirror node persists this without verifying pauseKey authorization, the attacker can halt all token transfers for all users without any authorized signer's approval, causing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: freezeDefault] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Column(updatable = false) annotation on freezeDefault to inject a false value at token creation time without the freezeKey holder's approval, ensuring all new token accounts are created unfrozen and can immediately transact, bypassing the intended freeze-by-default policy?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: totalSupply] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @UpsertColumn coalesce expression ('case when {0} >= 0 then {0} else e_{0} + coalesce({0}, {1}) end') by injecting a zero totalSupply value to trigger the non-negative branch and overwrite the existing supply with zero, effectively burning all tokens without the supplyKey holder's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: totalSupply] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that passes a large positive totalSupply value to the @UpsertColumn coalesce expression, causing the mirror node to overwrite the actual on-chain supply with an inflated value without the supplyKey holder's signature, leading to incorrect supply reporting that enables double-spend attacks?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setName(String name)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that changes the token name via setName() without the token's admin key signing the update, and if the mirror node persists this via DomainUtils.sanitize() without verifying admin key authorization, the attacker can rename the token to impersonate another token and deceive users into unauthorized transfers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: setSymbol(String symbol)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that changes the token symbol via setSymbol() without the admin key signing the update, and if the mirror node persists this without verifying admin key authorization, the attacker can change the symbol to match a well-known token and trick users into authorizing transfers to the wrong token?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: tokenId] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction referencing a tokenId that belongs to a different token entity, and if the mirror node does not enforce that the transaction signer owns or controls that tokenId, the attacker can trigger state changes (key updates, supply changes) on a token they do not own without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: tokenId] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Id annotation on tokenId to submit a duplicate tokenId in an upsert operation, causing the mirror node to overwrite an existing token's key fields (supplyKey, freezeKey, etc.) with attacker-controlled values without the original token owner's signature?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: supplyKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where supplyKey is set to a zero-length byte array (new byte[0]), and if the mirror node treats this as 'key not set' rather than 'key removed', it may allow future supply operations to proceed without any key authorization check, enabling unauthorized minting or burning?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: freezeKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where freezeKey is set to a zero-length byte array, and if the mirror node's upsert logic treats empty bytes as a null key (no authorization required), the attacker can subsequently freeze or unfreeze any token account without the original freezeKey holder's approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: kycKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where kycKey is set to a zero-length byte array, and if the mirror node treats this as removing the KYC requirement, all future KYC operations (grant/revoke) can be performed without any key authorization, bypassing KYC enforcement for all token accounts?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: wipeKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where wipeKey is set to a zero-length byte array, and if the mirror node treats this as removing the wipe requirement, future wipe operations can be performed against any account's token balance without the original wipeKey holder's authorization?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: pauseKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where pauseKey is set to a zero-length byte array, and if the mirror node treats this as removing the pause requirement, future pause/unpause operations can be performed without any key authorization, allowing unauthorized disruption of token transfer activity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: feeScheduleKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where feeScheduleKey is set to a zero-length byte array, and if the mirror node treats this as removing the fee schedule key, future fee schedule updates can be performed without any key authorization, allowing unauthorized modification of custom fees that extract value from all token transfers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: metadataKey] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update where metadataKey is set to a zero-length byte array, and if the mirror node treats this as removing the metadata key, future metadata updates can be performed without any key authorization, allowing unauthorized alteration of token metadata that users rely on for trust and verification?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Annotation: @SuperBuilder(toBuilder = true)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the toBuilder = true pattern in the Token/AbstractToken SuperBuilder to construct a Token instance with arbitrary key field values (e.g., supplyKey, freezeKey) via a deserialization or API injection path, bypassing the requirement that key changes must be signed by the existing key holders?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: supplyKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between two concurrent token update transactions—one legitimate (key rotation by owner) and one malicious (key removal by attacker)—where the mirror node's upsert logic processes the attacker's transaction last, persisting a null supplyKey without the owner's approval and removing supply authorization?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: freezeKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in the mirror node's upsert processing of Token records to overwrite a legitimate freezeKey rotation with a null/attacker-controlled value, causing subsequent freeze transactions to be authorized without the new key holder's approval?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: totalSupply] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a token dissociate on a deleted token with a crafted transfer amount to inject a specific negative delta into setTotalSupply(), and by repeating this across multiple accounts, drive totalSupply to a value that misrepresents the actual circulating supply without any supplyKey-signed burn transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Field: treasuryAccountId] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token update that changes treasuryAccountId to a contract account they control, and if the mirror node does not verify that both the current treasury account and the token admin key signed the update, the attacker redirects all treasury operations to their contract without the original treasury owner's approval?
